messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "nl-selfbuild-hollows-second-geography",
 "title": "The best-triangulated geography is the trap: NL's 28% sits on a capped colo base while its growth migrates to self-build architectures a regional entrant can't quote into",
 "narrative": "geo.NL is the model's most reassuring node — Statista and the Dutch Data Center Association agree within 3%. But that triangulation validates yesterday's colocation stock, not the composition of forward demand. The Netherlands is one of four countries holding ~60% of Europe's hyperscale self-build capacity, and self-build is growing 24% YoY versus colo's 19% — while Amsterdam (≈70% of NL colo) is supply-constrained. So the venture's second-largest geography weight (28% of the base) rests on a slice where the PDU-native part is capped and the growing part procures through hyperscaler global frameworks and busway/shelf architectures that a pre-launch regional entrant cannot reach in its first 12 months. The MW totals will keep rising; the biddable NL demand won't.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "geo.NL"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "NL is among the four countries concentrating ~60% of Europe's operational hyperscale self-build capacity, and European self-build additions grow 24% YoY — in NL, forward capacity growth increasingly happens outside the 951MW colo base that geo.NL's 0.28 apportionment measures",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c14",
    "digest:seg-mix#c12",
    "ledger:geo.NL"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Self-build capacity procures power distribution through hyperscaler global specs rather than regional channels, while the colo segment — larger but slower at 19% YoY and slowly losing share of total capacity — is the only NL demand a new CE entrant can actually bid; Amsterdam supply constraints cap even that",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c13",
    "digest:seg-mix#c18"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "geo.NL 0.28 overstates the entrant-reachable Dutch slice during the pre-launch first-12-months window: the ratio is anchored to a stalling colo stock while the growth it implicitly promises accrues to out-of-category, out-of-channel self-build",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.NL",
    "profile:Stage"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "geo.NL carries the ledger's cleanest triangulation (two independent sources within 3%), so it reads as the one geography nobody needs to re-examine. Agreement between sources measuring the same colo stock says nothing about whether that stock is the demand a Year-1 entrant will face — the precision of the snapshot hides the composition shift.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "2026 NL capacity-addition evidence (DDCA annual report plus fit-out tenders) showing new Dutch MW — including hyperscale campuses — specifying rack PDUs procured through channels open to regional vendors at rates proportional to NL's 28% weight.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "The self-build concentration and growth differential are directly sourced and Amsterdam's constraints are well documented; the offset is that NL retail/wholesale colo refresh demand is real and PDU-native, so the question is degree — whether reachable NL demand supports 0.28 or something closer to 0.20–0.22 of the base",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.NL",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.22,
   "note": "NL weight corrected to the colo-anchored, entrant-reachable slice; self-build growth and capped Amsterdam supply removed from the effective ratio"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "NL colocation capacity growth vs hyperscale self-build additions",
   "where": "Dutch Data Center Association 'State of the Dutch Data Centers' annual report; CBRE Amsterdam market quarterlies",
   "threshold": "NL colo net additions <5% YoY while national self-build MW grows >20%",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Grid-connection allocations for new NL data-center projects",
   "where": "Liander/TenneT congestion and connection-queue publications for the Amsterdam metro and Middenmeer/Eemshaven zones",
   "threshold": "New allocations flowing predominantly to hyperscale campus projects rather than colo expansions",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Build a bottom-up NL reachable-demand model: named colo operators × refresh cycle × racks, replacing the top-down capacity ratio for the second-largest geography",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "geo.NL",
   "note": "Converts a stock-based apportionment into a biddable-demand figure before NL channel investment is committed"
  },
  {
   "action": "Sequence market entry DE-first and treat NL as an Amsterdam retail-colo refresh play rather than a proportional 28% of pipeline effort",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Germany's larger, enterprise-heavy base is less exposed to the self-build migration in the Year-1 window"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "boundary-substitution",
 "riskId": "risk.boundary-substitution.nl-selfbuild-hollows-second-geography"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
